ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class; no specific entry for Chinese boiled pork-shrimp-mushroom dumpling. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJL covers boiled dumplings generically; no sub-code specific to pork-shrimp-mushroom filling. Closest defensible code used.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Chinese boiled dumplings is approximately 12 pieces, weighing around 240 g, typically consumed as a main meal at lunch or dinner.

nutrient_source.title: 猪肉三鲜水饺 Chinese Dumplings Stuffed With Minced Pork, Diced Shrimp And Mushroom
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) for this dish. All per-100g macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from HPB with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 1.2 g/100g based on HPB compositional data for similar pork-shrimp dumpling dishes; dietary fibre estimated at 1.2 g/100g from HPB analogous records. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins not reported by HPB for this entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — boiled wheat dumplings / pasta analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific pork-shrimp-mushroom dumpling. GI of 48 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled wheat-flour dumplings / filled pasta (GI range 39–55, representative value 48). The protein-rich filling (8.71 g/100g) and boiled preparation further moderate glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 48 × (8.03 × 2.4) / 100 = 48 × 19.27 / 100 ≈ 9.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg/100g with supportive potassium at 82 mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Boiled pork-shrimp-mushroom dumplings are suitable for hypertensive individuals at 46 mg sodium per 100 g; however, patients should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~48), moderate carbohydrate (8.03 g/100g), high protein (8.71 g/100g) slowing gastric emptying
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycaemic index (~48) and high protein content from pork, shrimp, and mushroom filling slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should practise portion control (limit to 8–10 pieces per meal) and avoid sugar-containing dipping sauces to prevent glycaemic load accumulation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.2 g/100g), low sodium (46 mg/100g), boiled preparation avoiding added fats
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Boiled preparation keeps saturated fat low (~1.2 g/100g) and avoids the additional fat and AGE formation associated with pan-frying; shrimp contributes modest omega-3 fatty acids, and the low sodium content supports cardiovascular health, though patients should strictly limit soy-sauce-based condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contributes arachidonic acid (pro-inflammatory omega-6); partially offset by shrimp omega-3 and mushroom beta-glucans
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should moderate portion size of these dumplings due to pork-derived arachidonic acid, prefer boiled over pan-fried preparation to avoid AGE formation, and pair with anti-inflammatory foods to balance the omega-6 load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 猪肉三鲜水饺 (Zhūròu sānxiān shuǐjiǎo) are traditional Chinese boiled dumplings filled with a savoury mixture of minced pork, diced shrimp (prawns), and shiitake or button mushrooms, enclosed in a thin wheat-flour wrapper. The name 'san xian' (三鲜) literally means 'three fresh ingredients', referring to the trio of pork, shrimp, and mushroom. The dumplings are boiled in water until the wrappers become tender and slightly translucent, yielding a soft, chewy texture with a juicy, umami-rich filling. They are commonly served with a dipping sauce of soy sauce, black vinegar, sesame oil, and chilli. In Singapore, this dish is a popular lunch and dinner option available at Chinese restaurants and hawker stalls. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate, with very low sodium at 46 mg. The high protein content from three animal and fungal sources, combined with a low glycaemic index (~48), makes this dish suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension when consumed without high-sodium dipping sauces. The boiled preparation avoids added fats and minimises advanced glycation end-product (AGE) formation, supporting cardiovascular health. Iron content is notable at 1.79 mg/100g, contributed by both pork and shrimp.

GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat-flour dumplings.

GI of 48 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat-flour dumplings/filled pasta; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 4.
